title: Community Reinvestment Act of 1977
summary: The law directed federal banking regulators to encourage insured depository institutions to help meet the credit needs of the communities they serve, including low- and moderate-income neighborhoods, consistent with safe and sound operations.
outcome_summary: The law became one of the major federal tools used to challenge redlining and press for fairer access to credit in communities that included many Black neighborhoods.
impact_notes: Closely tied to anti-redlining and equitable access to credit, though enforcement strength has varied over time.

sources.notes: Agency overview of CRA purpose and scope

title: Civil Rights Restoration Act of 1987
summary: The law restored broad institution-wide coverage for Title VI, Title IX, Section 504, and the Age Discrimination Act after narrower Supreme Court interpretations had limited their reach.
outcome_summary: By restoring broad Title VI coverage, the law reinforced federal civil-rights enforcement in federally funded institutions that could affect Black students, employees, and service recipients.
impact_notes: Important statutory restoration of civil-rights coverage after restrictive court interpretation.

scores.notes: Broadened the reach of anti-discrimination protections tied to federal funding.

sources.source_title: Civil Rights Restoration Act of 1987, Public Law 100-259

title: McCleskey v. Kemp
summary: The Supreme Court rejected an equal-protection and Eighth Amendment challenge to Georgia's death penalty despite statistical evidence of racial disparities, requiring proof of intentional discrimination in the individual case.
outcome_summary: The ruling made it substantially harder to use systemic statistical evidence to challenge racial disparities in criminal punishment.
impact_notes: Widely regarded as a major barrier to race-disparity challenges in criminal justice.

scores.notes: A major anti-equity precedent in criminal justice.

title: Fair Housing Amendments Act of 1988
summary: The law expanded the Fair Housing Act's protected classes and strengthened federal enforcement mechanisms and remedies for housing discrimination.
outcome_summary: The law strengthened federal enforcement of fair-housing protections in a policy area where Black Americans had long faced entrenched discrimination.
impact_notes: A significant strengthening of fair-housing enforcement after the 1968 act.

scores.notes: Expanded enforcement tools for housing discrimination claims.

sources.source_title: Fair Housing Amendments Act of 1988, Public Law 100-430

title: Texas Department of Housing and Community Affairs v. Inclusive Communities Project, Inc.
summary: The Supreme Court held that disparate-impact claims are cognizable under the Fair Housing Act, preserving an important tool for challenging facially neutral housing policies with discriminatory effects.
outcome_summary: The ruling preserved a key legal avenue for challenging housing practices that reinforce segregation or disadvantage Black communities without explicit racial language.
impact_notes: Important modern fair-housing precedent protecting disparate-impact claims.

scores.notes: Preserved disparate-impact enforcement under the Fair Housing Act.

sources.source_title: Texas Department of Housing and Community Affairs v. Inclusive Communities Project, Inc., 576 U.S. 519 (2015)
